# PipelineIQ — Context for Claude

<!-- generated by init-claude-md -->
## What this project does

Claude Code slash commands that form a closed-loop **spec → plan → implement → audit → fix → merge** pipeline. Not a library or app — the deliverables are markdown prompt files in `commands/` that Claude Code executes as slash commands. There is no build step, no test suite, and no runtime beyond Claude Code itself.

<!-- generated by init-claude-md -->
## Invariants

1. **Artifact directories are never inside worktrees.** `specs/`, `Implementation Plans/`, `Working Logs/`, and `learnings.md` exist only in the main repo root. Subagent reference files use `[MASTER_REPO_PATH]` to write there — if a subagent writes an artifact relative to the worktree, the pipeline's next phase won't find it.
2. **`specs/` is an inbox; `specs/applied/` is an archive; `specs/team/` is the batch-ready staging area.** A spec moves to `applied/` only after `impl-plan` saves a plan for it (or after `/spec-splitter` generates children from it). `pipeline-team` discovers pending work by listing `specs/team/` by default (or an explicit directory via argument). Moving a spec prematurely makes it invisible to the batch runner.
3. **`references/` files are subagent prompt templates, not documentation.** Each pipeline phase reads its reference file at runtime and fills `[VARIABLES]`. Renaming, moving, or reformatting a reference file breaks the phase that loads it — the pre-flight check in `pipeline.md` validates all 6 exist before starting.
4. **`learnings.md` is append-only during pipeline runs.** Pipeline Phase 6 appends entries; it never deletes or rewrites existing ones. `/learnings-review` is the only process that modifies existing entries.

<!-- generated by init-claude-md -->
## Contracts

1. **SKIP_MERGE output format** — `pipeline.md` and `pipeline-team.md` share a structured contract: when `SKIP_MERGE=true`, pipeline Phase 6 must output `PIPELINE_COMPLETE spec=<path> worktree=<path> branch=<name> working_log=<path> audit=<path> remaining_errors=<N>`. The lead in `pipeline-team.md` parses this exact format. Both files carry matching contract markers — changing one without the other breaks teammate coordination.
2. **PIPELINE_FAILURE format** — teammates report `PIPELINE_FAILURE spec=<path> worktree=<path> branch=<name> working_log=<path> reason=<description>`. The lead's failure-handling protocol (`references/pipeline-team/failure-handling.md`) depends on this structure.
3. **Audit → Fix handoff** — the "Actionable Errors" section in audit documents is the machine-readable interface between `/audit-implementation` and `/fix`. Every error in "Failures & Root Causes" must appear either as a numbered actionable error or in the "not actionable" list — omitting from both is a silent data loss.
4. **Worktree commit hook caveat** — bare `git commit` inside a worktree is intercepted by a harness hook that reads the main repo CWD (always on master) and blocks. Both `impl.md` and `fix.md` document the workaround: use `git -C <worktree-path> commit`.
5. **Keyword-stopwords contract** — `pipeline-team.md` Phase 1 and `spec-splitter.md` Phase 3 share the same keyword extraction rules: words > 4 chars, same stopword list, 2+ keyword match threshold. Both files carry `[CONTRACT: keyword-stopwords]` markers. Changing the stopword list or threshold in one without the other causes spec-splitter's wiring verification to pass but pipeline-team's dependency detection to disagree.

<!-- generated by init-claude-md -->
## Gotchas

1. **SKIP_MERGE must be a whitespace-delimited token.** `pipeline.md` splits arguments on whitespace and checks for an exact match. Embedding it inside a filename (e.g., `my-spec-SKIP_MERGE=true.md`) won't trigger the flag — the pipeline will try to find a spec file with that name and fail.
2. **`pipeline-team` hardcodes the commands path.** The teammate spawn prompt contains `/mnt/c/Users/Epkone/.claude/commands/` as a literal path with a `~/.claude/commands/` fallback. After installing to a different location, this path must be updated or teammates won't find `pipeline.md`.
3. **Stale worktree state blocks `pipeline-team` startup.** Pre-flight Check 4 refuses to run if any non-main worktree exists. A forgotten worktree from a previous session (including crashed ones) will block all batch runs until cleaned up with `git worktree remove` + `git worktree prune`.
4. **Re-audit after fix is conditional.** If the fixer logs "0 errors fixed" or applies no file changes, pipeline Phase 5 skips the re-audit subagent entirely. A fixer that silently fails without logging will cause the pipeline to skip re-audit and proceed with the original error count.

<!-- generated by init-claude-md -->
## Commands

Install: copy the entire `commands/` directory (including `references/`) to `~/.claude/commands/` or `<repo>/.claude/commands/`. Both subdirectories under `references/` (`pipeline/` and `pipeline-team/`) must be present — `pipeline.md` validates all 6 phase files at startup; `pipeline-team.md` validates its 2 reference files when those phases run.
